records.ety: [L. injunctio, fr. injungere, injunctum, to join into, to enjoin. See Enjoin.]

defs: The act of enjoining; the act of directing, commanding, or prohibiting.
That which is enjoined; an order; a mandate; a decree; a command; a precept; a direction.
quotes: For still they knew, and ought to have still remembered, The high injunction, not to taste that fruit.
Necessary as the injunctions of lawful authority.

defs: A writ or process, granted by a court of equity, and, in some cases, under statutes, by a court of law, whereby a party is required to do or to refrain from doing certain acts, according to the exigency of the writ.
notes: ☞ It is more generally used as a preventive than as a restorative process, although by no means confined to the former.
